description: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control.

subcriteria.name: policy_communication
subcriteria.reference_text: Security policies and procedures published on company intranet, wiki, or documentation platform like Confluence, Notion, or SharePoint. Employees can access policies covering access control, data handling, incident reporting, and acceptable use.

subcriteria.name: security_awareness
subcriteria.reference_text: Security awareness training delivered annually to all employees. Quarterly security updates or newsletters communicated via email or Slack. Training covers phishing, social engineering, password hygiene, and reporting procedures. Attendance tracked.

subcriteria.name: management_communication
subcriteria.reference_text: Quarterly board meetings and annual meetings where security status, risks, and compliance are discussed. Meeting minutes document key decisions. Incident escalation procedures communicated to all staff.

subcriteria.name: whistleblower_channel
subcriteria.reference_text: Anonymous reporting channel such as whistleblower hotline available to employees. Separate communication channel for reporting concerns when normal channels are ineffective.

mismatch_signals.reason: Vulnerability scanning is security testing, not internal communication.
Backup procedures are not internal communication of objectives.
